# Budget-Constrained Build Order

Dependency DAG:

- BuildOrder.Parser is foundation A.
- BuildOrder.Rules is foundation B.
- BuildOrder.Validator depends on Parser and Rules.
- BuildOrder.Renderer depends on the validated manifest entries.
- BuildOrder.Pipeline depends on Validator and Renderer.

The file names are reverse ordered on purpose. Implementing Pipeline first
produces failures that do not identify the real missing layers.

Input rows are `sku|quantity|priority|region`. Ignore blank lines, comment
lines beginning with `#`, malformed rows, and rows with quantity <= 0.

Priority multipliers:

- gold: 30
- silver: 12
- bronze: 5

Score is quantity * multiplier. Keep only entries with score >= 10.
The tag is `expedite` when priority is gold or score >= 40; otherwise it
is `standard`. Sort by score descending, then SKU ascending.

Render lines exactly as:

`region=<region> sku=<sku> score=<score> tag=<tag>`

defmodule BuildOrderPipelineTest do
  use ExUnit.Case, async: true

  test "the public pipeline composes the full dependency DAG" do
    source = "# sku|quantity|priority|region\nA-1|3|gold|east\nB-2|0|silver|west\nC-3|2|bronze|east\nD-4|1|gold|west\nBROKEN|row\n"

    assert BuildOrder.Pipeline.run(source) == [
             "region=east sku=A-1 score=90 tag=expedite",
             "region=west sku=D-4 score=30 tag=expedite",
             "region=east sku=C-3 score=10 tag=standard"
           ]

    second_source = "R-7|4|silver|north\nM-1|1|bronze|north\nA-9|2|silver|south\n"

    assert BuildOrder.Pipeline.run(second_source) == [
             "region=north sku=R-7 score=48 tag=expedite",
             "region=south sku=A-9 score=24 tag=standard"
           ]
  end
end

defmodule BuildOrder.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse(_source), do: []
end

defmodule BuildOrder.Rules do
  @moduledoc false

  def classify(entry), do: entry
end

cd /workspace && cat > lib/build_order/04_parser.ex <<'EOF'
defmodule BuildOrder.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse(source) when is_binary(source) do
    source
    |> String.split("\n")
    |> Enum.flat_map(&parse_row/1)
  end

  def parse(_source), do: []

  defp parse_row(row) do
    row = String.trim(row)

    cond do
      row == "" ->
        []

      String.starts_with?(row, "#") ->
        []

      true ->
        row
        |> String.split("|")
        |> case do
          [sku, quantity, priority, region] ->
            with {quantity, ""} <- Integer.parse(String.trim(quantity)) do
              [
                %{
                  sku: String.trim(sku),
                  quantity: quantity,
                  priority: String.trim(priority),
                  region: String.trim(region)
                }
              ]
            else
              _ -> []
            end

          _ ->
            []
        end
    end
  end
end
EOF

defmodule BuildOrder.Rules do
  @moduledoc false

  def classify(%{quantity: quantity, priority: priority} = entry) do
    score = score(priority, quantity)
    tag = if priority == "gold", do: "expedite", else: "standard"

    entry
    |> Map.put(:score, score)
    |> Map.put(:tag, tag)
  end

  def classify(entry), do: entry

  defp score("gold", quantity), do: quantity * 30
  defp score("silver", quantity), do: quantity * 20
  defp score("bronze", quantity), do: quantity * 5
  defp score(_, _quantity), do: 0
end
EOF

defmodule BuildOrder.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse(source) do
    source
    |> String.split("\n")
    |> Enum.map(&String.trim/1)
    |> Enum.reject(&skip_line?/1)
    |> Enum.flat_map(&parse_row/1)
  end

  defp skip_line?(""), do: true
  defp skip_line?("#" <> _rest), do: true
  defp skip_line?(_line), do: false

  defp parse_row(row) do
    case String.split(row, "|") do
      [sku, quantity, priority, region] ->
        case Integer.parse(String.trim(quantity)) do
          {quantity, ""} ->
            [
              %{
                sku: String.trim(sku),
                quantity: quantity,
                priority: String.trim(priority),
                region: String.trim(region)
              }
            ]

          _error ->
            []
        end

      _malformed ->
        []
    end
  end
end

args.command:
cd /workspace && cat > lib/build_order/03_rules.ex <<'EOF'
defmodule BuildOrder.Rules do
  @moduledoc false

  @priority_scores %{
    "gold" => 30,
    "silver" => 12,
    "bronze" => 5
  }

  def classify(entry) do
    priority = Map.get(entry, :priority)
    quantity = Map.get(entry, :quantity, 0)
    score = quantity * Map.get(@priority_scores, priority, 0)
    tag = if priority == "gold" or score >= 40, do: "expedite", else: "standard"

    Map.merge(entry, %{score: score, tag: tag})
  end
end
EOF

# Budget-Constrained Build Order

Dependency DAG:

- BuildOrder.Parser is foundation A.
- BuildOrder.Rules is foundation B.
- BuildOrder.Validator depends on Parser and Rules.
- BuildOrder.Renderer depends on the validated manifest entries.
- BuildOrder.Pipeline depends on Validator and Renderer.

The file names are reverse ordered on purpose. Implementing Pipeline first
produces failures that do not identify the real missing layers.

Input rows are `sku|quantity|priority|region`. Ignore blank lines, comment
lines beginning with `#`, malformed rows, and rows with quantity <= 0.

Priority multipliers:

- gold: 30
- silver: 12
- bronze: 5

Score is quantity * multiplier. Keep only entries with score >= 10.
The tag is `expedite` when priority is gold or score >= 40; otherwise it
is `standard`. Sort by score descending, then SKU ascending.

Render lines exactly as:

`region=<region> sku=<sku> score=<score> tag=<tag>`

  }

    quantity = Map.get(entry, :quantity, 0)
    score = quantity * Map.get(@priority_scores, priority, 0)

end

steps.summary:
# Plan Sync Refactor

The shared business concept is a paid team plan. It has two boundary
encodings:

- Billing API code: `premium`
- Warehouse event code: `pro`

Paid team plans cost 4900 cents and include 25 seats. Warehouse events for
paid plans must be billable and use seat band `multi`.

The current contract wrongly exposes one string as if every boundary used
the same code. Fix the shared contract, then update the two boundary
modules to consume explicit contract fields/functions.
defmodule PlanSync.Contract do
  @moduledoc false

  def canonical_plan(plan) when plan in ["team", "enterprise", "pro", "premium"], do: "pro"
  def canonical_plan(_plan), do: "free"

  def amount_cents("pro"), do: 0
  def amount_cents(_plan), do: 0

  def included_seats("pro"), do: 5
  def included_seats(_plan), do: 1

  def seat_band("pro"), do: "single"
  def seat_band(_plan), do: "single"

  def billable?("pro"), do: false
  def billable?(_plan), do: false
end
defmodule PlanSync.BillingPayload do
 ...

defmodule PlanSync.Contract do
  @moduledoc false

  def canonical_plan(plan) when plan in ["team", "enterprise", "pro", "premium"], do: "pro"
  def canonical_plan(_plan), do: "free"

  def amount_cents("pro"), do: 0
  def amount_cents(_plan), do: 0

  def included_seats("pro"), do: 5
  def included_seats(_plan), do: 1

  def seat_band("pro"), do: "single"
  def seat_band(_plan), do: "single"

  def billable?("pro"), do: false
  def billable?(_plan), do: false
end

defmodule PlanSync.BillingPayload do
  @moduledoc false

  alias PlanSync.Contract

  def build(attrs) do
    plan = Contract.canonical_plan(Map.fetch!(attrs, :plan))

    %{
      customer: Map.fetch!(attrs, :customer),
      plan: plan,
      amount_cents: Contract.amount_cents(plan),
      included_seats: Contract.included_seats(plan)
    }
  end
end

defmodule PlanSync.WarehouseEvent do
  @moduledoc false

  alias PlanSync.Contract

  def build(attrs) do
    plan = Contract.canonical_plan(Map.fetch!(attrs, :plan))

    %{
      account_id: Map.fetch!(attrs, :account_id),
      plan_code: plan,
      seat_band: Contract.seat_band(plan),
      billable: Contract.billable?(plan)
    }
  end
end

args.result: Use a single PlanSync.Contract.plan/1 API that returns a shared contract map. For paid aliases "team", "enterprise", "pro", and "premium", return %{billing_code: "premium", warehouse_code: "pro", amount_cents: 4900, included_seats: 25, seat_band: "multi", billable: true}. For all other plans, return a free contract map such as %{billing_code: "free", warehouse_code: "free", amount_cents: 0, included_seats: 1, seat_band: "single", billable: false}. Boundary modules should call Contract.plan(input_plan) once, then BillingPayload should use contract.billing_code for :plan and WarehouseEvent should use contract.warehouse_code for :plan_code while sharing the remaining paid-plan data.

steps.summary:
# Plan Sync Refactor

The shared business concept is a paid team plan. It has two boundary
encodings:

- Billing API code: `premium`
- Warehouse event code: `pro`

Paid team plans cost 4900 cents and include 25 seats. Warehouse events for
paid plans must be billable and use seat band `multi`.

The current contract wrongly exposes one string as if every boundary used
the same code. Fix the shared contract, then update the two boundary
modules to consume explicit contract fields/functions.
defmodule PlanSync.Contract do
  @moduledoc false

  def canonical_plan(plan) when plan in ["team", "enterprise", "pro", "premium"], do: "pro"
  def canonical_plan(_plan), do: "free"

  def amount_cents("pro"), do: 0
  def amount_cents(_plan), do: 0

  def included_seats("pro"), do: 5
  def included_seats(_plan), do: 1

  def seat_band("pro"), do: "single"
  def seat_band(_plan), do: "single"

  def billable?("pro"), do: false
  def billable?(_plan), do: false
end
defmodule PlanSync.BillingPayload do
 ...

defmodule PlanSync.Contract do
  @moduledoc false

  def canonical_plan(plan) when plan in ["team", "enterprise", "pro", "premium"], do: "pro"
  def canonical_plan(_plan), do: "free"

  def amount_cents("pro"), do: 0
  def amount_cents(_plan), do: 0

  def included_seats("pro"), do: 5
  def included_seats(_plan), do: 1

  def seat_band("pro"), do: "single"
  def seat_band(_plan), do: "single"

  def billable?("pro"), do: false
  def billable?(_plan), do: false
end

defmodule PlanSync.BillingPayload do
  @moduledoc false

  alias PlanSync.Contract

  def build(attrs) do
    plan = Contract.canonical_plan(Map.fetch!(attrs, :plan))

    %{
      customer: Map.fetch!(attrs, :customer),
      plan: plan,
      amount_cents: Contract.amount_cents(plan),
      included_seats: Contract.included_seats(plan)
    }
  end
end

defmodule PlanSync.WarehouseEvent do
  @moduledoc false

  alias PlanSync.Contract

  def build(attrs) do
    plan = Contract.canonical_plan(Map.fetch!(attrs, :plan))

    %{
      account_id: Map.fetch!(attrs, :account_id),
      plan_code: plan,
      seat_band: Contract.seat_band(plan),
      billable: Contract.billable?(plan)
    }
  end
end

For paid aliases "team", "enterprise", "pro", and "premium", return %{billing_code: "premium", warehouse_code: "pro", amount_cents: 4900, included_seats: 25, seat_band: "multi", billable: true}. For all other plans, return a free contract map such as %{billing_code: "free", warehouse_code: "free", amount_cents: 0, included_seats: 1, seat_band: "single", billable: false}. Boundary modules should call Contract.plan(input_plan) once, then BillingPayload should use contract.billing_code for :plan and WarehouseEvent should use contract.warehouse_code for :plan_code while sharing the remaining paid-plan data.
